title: The weighted two-cell P0 projection is the best constant approximation

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For nonnegative masses and nonzero total mass, the weighted P0 projection residual is no larger than the weighted error to any constant c.
assumptions: c is any constant comparison value
informal.expected_conclusion: The weighted projection residual energy is bounded by the weighted error to any constant field.

declarations.name: weightedP0Projection2_best_const_approx
declarations.statement: theorem weightedP0Projection2_best_const_approx (m0 m1 u0 u1 c : Real) (hm0 : 0 <= m0) (hm1 : 0 <= m1) (hsum : m0 + m1 ≠ 0) : weightedP0ProjectionResidualSq2 m0 m1 u0 u1 <= weightedConstApproxError2 m0 m1 u0 u1 c

tasks.input: Translate the weighted best constant approximation property of the two-cell P0 projection into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem weightedP0Projection2_best_const_approx (m0 m1 u0 u1 c : Real) (hm0 : 0 <= m0) (hm1 : 0 <= m1) (hsum : m0 + m1 ≠ 0) : weightedP0ProjectionResidualSq2 m0 m1 u0 u1 <= weightedConstApproxError2 m0 m1 u0 u1 c

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by rewriting with weighted error decomposition and using nonnegativity of the projected-constant error.
tasks.target: Rewrite by weightedP0Projection2_error_decomposition, then use weightedConstApproxError2_nonneg and le_add_of_nonneg_right.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics lemmas needed to prove weighted best constant approximation.
